reasoning: The January 2025 Interim Final Rule establishing comprehensive export controls on AI model weights and advanced computing items, originally set for compliance on May 15, 2025, was officially paused/rescinded on May 13, 2025 by the Bureau of Industry and Security. The administration opted to abandon worldwide license requirements in favor of a non-binding "due diligence" framework for AI technologies, signaling a major policy shift. As of November 23, 2025, there are no replacement "export controls" published in the Federal Register, and lawmakers are instead advocating for proposed legislation (the Chip Security Act) to handle chip tracking. With only five weeks left in 2025 and an explicitly deregulatory stance from the executive branch on this issue, it is highly unlikely any new broad export controls satisfying the criteria will be enacted by year's end.
